text: Fracture rehabilitation should follow the treating clinician's restrictions. Early mobilization may include gentle, pain-limited range-of-motion work when the treating team has cleared it. Stop and seek clinical advice for increasing pain, new numbness, colour change, marked swelling, wound drainage, or other concerning symptoms.

Progression after ankle fracture depends on healing, imaging, weight-bearing instructions, and symptoms. Strengthening should be gradual and conditional on the treating clinician's protocol. Do not infer readiness for impact activity from time alone. New calf swelling, severe pain, weakness, or colour change requires prompt clinical assessment.
